methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
OSH Cut, a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb Chamberlain, presents a valuation challenge due to its early stage and limited comparable universe. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes 11 peers across public and private markets—1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms—but the peer-median method is inapplicable here. No same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables exist in sufficient density (minimum 3 required); the narrow filters yielded zero datapoints despite an 11-company sector backdrop, making sector-wide median benchmarking uninformative and potentially misleading.

The identifiable private peer set includes 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, though stage and valuation dispersion among these firms limits direct applicability. Without sufficient peers at bootstrapped stage or comparable scale, traditional triangulation methods cannot be reliably deployed.

Given the methodological constraints, valuation of OSH Cut cannot be supported by quantitative comparison at this time. Any valuation framework would depend on forward-looking metrics (revenue traction, unit economics, capital efficiency post-bootstrapping) rather than peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue run rate, margin profile, and capital requirements at next funding stage. Until OSH Cut reaches measurable scale or more comparable bootstrapped-stage robotics ventures enter the dataset, peer-based valuation remains structurally unavailable.
